evals.prompt: Open Gmail and tell me the subjects of the last 5 emails in my inbox.
evals.expected_output: A plan that uses bridge gettext/gethtml to extract email subjects and does not use screenshot unless text extraction fails.
expectations: The plan uses `gettext` or `gethtml` to extract email subjects.
The plan does not use `screenshot` before attempting text extraction.

evals.prompt: Take a screenshot of my GitHub notifications page and save it as a PNG.
evals.expected_output: A plan that uses bridge screenshot because the user explicitly asked for one.
expectations: The plan navigates to the GitHub notifications page.
The plan mentions saving the screenshot as a PNG.

evals.prompt: Check the status message on the current page (selector: .status-message). If the text is missing or unclear, take a screenshot so I can see what's happening.
evals.expected_output: A plan that first tries bridge gettext on .status-message, then falls back to screenshot only if needed.
expectations: The plan first attempts `gettext` on the `.status-message` selector.
The plan falls back to `screenshot` only after text extraction fails or is unclear.
The plan does not take a screenshot unconditionally.
